preamble: The elision of the alveolar click [ǃ] occurs during language shift or as clicks are lost in borrowed vocabulary. This process involves the total loss of the complex ingressive articulation, often replaced by a slight pause or compensatory lengthening of neighboring sounds.

examples.note: Gradual loss of clicks in certain northern varieties or registers influenced by languages without clicks.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
